Which function gives the number of points awarded for a correct answer $x$ seconds from the time the question is asked, where $x$ is a multiple of 4?
questionText: For an online trivia game, 640 points are awarded for a correct answer if a question is answered in less than 4 seconds from the time the question is asked. subject: math
prompt:
$h(x) = - \frac{1}{2}x^{2} + 9x + 18$
The function $h$ gives the estimated number of students in a school organization $x$ years after the organization was established, where $0 \leq x \leq 12$. Which statement is the best interpretation of $h(4) = 46$?
questionText:
$h(x) = - \frac{1}{2}x^{2} + 9x + 18$
The function $h$ gives the estimated number of students in a school organization $x$ years after the organization was established, where $0 \leq x \leq 12$. Which statement is the best interpretation of $h(4) = 46$?

choices.text: It is estimated there were 4 students in the school organization when it was established.
It is estimated there were 46 students in the school organization when it was established.
It is estimated there were 46 students in the school organization 4 years after it was established.
It is estimated there were 46 students in the school organization 12 years after it was established.

subject: math
prompt: The function $t(x) = -4x + 68$ gives the estimated temperature, in degrees Fahrenheit, of a town $x$ hours after 7 p.m., where $0 \leq x \leq 6$. What is the best interpretation of -4 in this context?
questionText: The function $t(x) = -4x + 68$ gives the estimated temperature, in degrees Fahrenheit, of a town $x$ hours after 7 p.m., where $0 \leq x \leq 6$. What is the best interpretation of -4 in this context?

choices.text: The estimated temperature decreases 4 degrees Fahrenheit each hour.
The estimated temperature decreases 68 degrees Fahrenheit each hour.
At 7 p.m., the estimated temperature is -4 degrees Fahrenheit.
At 7 p.m., the estimated temperature is 6 degrees Fahrenheit.

subject: math
prompt: For a study, a group of squirrels will be selected from a habitat consisting of 240 squirrels, and a group of groundhogs will be selected from a habitat consisting of 210 groundhogs. Some of the squirrels and groundhogs will be in a treatment group, and some of the squirrels and groundhogs will be in a control group. Which of the following is necessary for this study to attempt to establish a cause-and-effect relationship between two variables?
questionText: For a study, a group of squirrels will be selected from a habitat consisting of 240 squirrels, and a group of groundhogs will be selected from a habitat consisting of 210 groundhogs. Some of the squirrels and groundhogs will be in a treatment group, and some of the squirrels and groundhogs will be in a control group. Which of the following is necessary for this study to attempt to establish a cause-and-effect relationship between two variables?

choices.text: The number of squirrels in the treatment group is equal to the number of groundhogs in the treatment group, and the number of squirrels in the control group is equal to the number of groundhogs in the control group.
The squirrels and the groundhogs are randomly assigned to the treatment and control groups.
The squirrels and the groundhogs are randomly selected from their respective habitats.

The average age of the squirrels in the treatment group is equal to the average age of the groundhogs in the treatment group, and the average age of the squirrels in the control group is equal to the average age of the groundhogs in the control group.

category.subject: Math

subject: math
prompt: A certain investment account offers a special interest rate for the first 4 months the account is open followed by a lower interest rate for the remainder of the time the account is open. Marisol opened one of these accounts with an original account balance of \$800.00 and did not make any other deposits or withdrawals. 4 months after Marisol opened the account, the balance had increased by 0.5% of the original balance. 6 months after Marisol opened the account, the balance had increased by an additional 0.2% of the balance at the end of the first 4 months. Every 2 months after the first 6 months, the balance had increased by an additional 0.2% of the balance 2 months before. Which of the following equations could represent the account balance $B(x)$, in dollars, $x$ months after the account was opened, where $x \geq 4$?
questionText: A certain investment account offers a special interest rate for the first 4 months the account is open followed by a lower interest rate for the remainder of the time the account is open. Marisol opened one of these accounts with an original account balance of \$800.00 and did not make any other deposits or withdrawals. 4 months after Marisol opened the account, the balance had increased by 0.5% of the original balance. 6 months after Marisol opened the account, the balance had increased by an additional 0.2% of the balance at the end of the first 4 months. Every 2 months after the first 6 months, the balance had increased by an additional 0.2% of the balance 2 months before. Which of the following equations could represent the account balance $B(x)$, in dollars, $x$ months after the account was opened, where $x \geq 4$?

questionText: The expression $105y^5 - 70y^4$ is equivalent to $ry^4 (15y - 10)$, where $r$ is a constant. What is the value of $r$?

subject: math
prompt:
$y=0.5x^{2}-7.5x+65$
The equation gives the estimated stock price $y$, in dollars, for a certain company $x$ days after the company went public, where $0 \leq x \lt 20$. Which statement is the best interpretation of $(x,y)=(2, 52)$ in this context?
questionText:
$y=0.5x^{2}-7.5x+65$
The equation gives the estimated stock price $y$, in dollars, for a certain company $x$ days after the company went public, where $0 \leq x \lt 20$. Which statement is the best interpretation of $(x,y)=(2, 52)$ in this context?

choices.text: Two days after the company went public, the company's estimated stock price is \$52.
Two days after the company went public, the company's estimated stock price is \$2.
The company's estimated stock price increased \$52 every day after the company went public.
The company's estimated stock price increased \$2 every 52 days after the company went public.
